question:
What topics are covered in IoT Networks, Architectures
  and Applications?
answer: The topics covered in IoT Networks, Architectures and Applications include basics of networking, OSI and TCP/IP models, addressing, and TCP connections; what is IoT and why is IoT; applications of IoT, potential, and challenges; IoT network architectures; types of communication; and network protocols and standards for IoT connectivity, among others. These topics are covered in the course syllabus, which outlines the various sections and their corresponding sessions, and are supported by the course handouts, including IoT Networks, Architectures and Applications-CSE 2023 Batch.pdf.

Course: IoT Networks, Architectures and Applications
Course Code: CSE3722
Credits: 03 (L-D-P: 3-0-0)
Section: Course Coordinator
Section Type: faculty

Dr. Sandeep Kumar
Email: sandeep.kumar@bmu.edu.in
Contact No: +91-9133872511
Office: 67, IV Floor, E2 Building

Aim of the course : This course is designed to provide students with solid technical knowledge and skills to
understand the Internet of Things (IoT) systems. The beginning portion focuses on the basic concepts,
architectures, applications and design challenges of the internet of things. The various topics on the IoT
protocols, gateway, computing layers, security and wireless sensing have been covered in the middle portion
of this course. The remaining part takes up the IoT circuits, embedded systems, case studies and a number of
online IoT platforms.
Course Outcomes (CO): At the end of the course the students should be able to do the following:
Course Outcome Statement Mapped
Component
Associated Activities / Assessment

concepts, network
architectures, applications and
design challenges in the
internet of things.
Employability  Intensive theory sessions.
 In-class tool demonstrations
 Industry case studies etc.
CO2 Analyse the functionality of
various computing paradigms,
sensor networks, IoT protocols
and security aspects.
Skill Development  Hands-on practical sessions in labs.
 Experiential learning on the simulation
platforms e.g. WokWi, Proteus, CupCarbon,
MATLAB Simulink etc.
 Data communication to the Android Apps
and cloud platforms.
CO3 Apply the acquired knowledge
to design IoT circuits and
systems.
Entrepreneurship,
Skill Development
 Getting familiar with cloud services such as
Blynk, ThingSpeak, HiveMQ, CloudMQTT,
Bolt, PushBullet etc.
 Design and development of the prototypes
based on the real world applications.

Prerequisite: if any
1. Basic knowledge of microcontroller boards.
2. Programming in Python and C.
